page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.55 GHz. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2677.3 in stress-ng single-core tests, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are average, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 21587.12 and cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 1033.55 GB/sec. However, memory write performance for large uncached blocks is relatively weak at 18.82 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance shows strong capabilities in text generation for small language models, achieving 307.15 tokens per second on a 135M parameter model.

The dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio make this instance a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring stable, non-shared compute resources without the premium of specialized memory or GPU hardware. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, software compilation, Redis database operations, and moderate text processing or lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7T83 x86_64 processor running at 2.55 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but provides a 7 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance and efficient text generation for small language models, alongside average multi-core and memory read capabilities, though memory write speeds for large blocks are weaker. The dedicated CPU allocation offers a stable, balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is highly suitable for web serving, software compilation, Redis deployments, and moderate database operations.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a stable memory-to-CPU ratio for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7T83 cores with 64 GB RAM.
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation.
7 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
KVM hypervisor-based architecture with dedicated resource allocation.
Suitable for web serving, software compilation, and Redis databases.
